title: Level 6.78
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 6.78: "Wherefore Bluffing"}}__NOTOC____NOEDITSECTION__[[Category:Class Mu]]
[[File:Level-6-78.png|center|link=]]

A photograph of Level 6.78, a sub-level between [[Level 6]] and [[Level 7]]. Though it has a significant resemblance to [[Level 6]], its properties are still barely understood, thus making it one of the most anomalous sub-levels of the Backrooms.

{{Background|file=dark6.png}}
LEVEL '''6.78'''
THREAT INDEX
Class Mu

'''Exit:''' 4/5
Exits Seldom
'''Environment:''' 5/5
Extremely Perilous Environment
'''Entities:''' ?/5
Entity Presence Indeterminable
'''Radiation: 5/5'''
Radioactive Exclusion Zone
[[File:289radiationbg.gif|2000px|center]]

Level 6.78 is a vast underground network of corridors, often described as “Wherefore Bluffing.” Navigation is difficult due to its twilight ambiance and maze-like structure, though some corridors may still contain dim, functioning lights. While the layout resembles utility halls, hospital equipment, including gurneys and IV stands, is also scattered throughout; additionally, barrels and containers that hold radioactive material can identified in various locations. All surfaces are heavily rusted and decaying, covered in mold and bacterial colonies due to the high humidity.
Occasionally, doors appear throughout the corridors. These lead to rooms that vary in size and function—some resemble large parking garages, while others are small storage rooms. Larger rooms are usually more open, offer multiple exits, and are more likely to have lighting. In contrast, smaller rooms appear ransacked, filled with chemical containers, rusty tools, and personal belongings. These confined spaces increase the risk of being trapped by hostile entities. Both corridors and rooms often contain crates similar to those found in [[Level 1]]. These crates, made of either wood or cardboard, are usually scattered across the floor. Their contents vary but often include [[Object 1|Almond Water]], chemical containers, drug-filled syringes, and pill bottles. Many of the drugs are nootropics, though reports suggest the presence of opioids as well. The [[Object 1|Almond Water]] found in these crates frequently causes Adderall-like effects and carries a risk of dependency. Spending even a short time at this level may lead to skin decay, minor tumors, and open wounds. Long-term exposure can result in necrosis, internal bleeding, and cancer—ultimately proving fatal. This phenomenon, known as “The Rot,” is believed to be connected to the widespread rust and decay throughout the level. Additionally, it is associated with the [[Phenomenon 35|Roth Syndrome]]. The rot and rust may result from an unexplained phenomenon within the level that seems to accelerate all forms of decay by distorting the fabric of space-time. There is speculation that Level 6.78 was once a research facility for an unknown groupSome documents found at the level contained unidentified logos, supporting the suggestion. and that a catastrophic experimentSome theories claim that a catastrophic experiment at this level led to the creation of “The Nucleigo” in [[Level 630]]. might have triggered its current state. While the entity presence is indeterminable in Level 6.78 due to exploration difficulties, wanderer statements confirm the presence of [[Phenomenon 35|Wretches]], Nuclear Membri from [[Level 630]],The presence of Nuclear Membri at this level supports theories that [[Object 3|Nuclear Infection]] was once active here. [[Entity 10|Skin-Stealers]], and [[Entity 8|Hounds]]. Some wanderer accounts suggest that the presence of undocumented entities is unique to this level. Entry and exit points are rare and typically involve rusted doors.Some wanderers reported exiting to [[Level 6]] by [[No-Clipping|no-clipping]] through a vantablack wall within Level 6.78, though this remains unverified. Although Level 6.78 is considered a sub-level of [[Level 6]], the fore-run is to be accessible from various other levels. While not officially categorized, entrances believed to appear on indoor levels with exits tend to lead to outdoor levels.

{{Footnotes}}



















[[File:Level6dot78new.jpeg|400px|center|link=]]




Life is a long journey, as if there is a thin thread between birth and death, and you try to maintain your balance. Even though you encounter many [[Entity List|obstacles]] and [[Object List|opportunities]] along the way, you walk with your eyes fixed on your goal like a [[Hounds|hungry dog]]...

Or I was walking. I guess there are [[M.E.G.|thousands of other fools]] like me who don't look around. My life has always been a meeting; I was the king, the sun, and the moon of my own world, as if I were the incarnation of God.

I never tasted the [[The Frontrooms|fruits of life because]], to live like kings, one must have great ambitions and find a banquet table that lasts a lifetime, but in the process, I destroyed my kingdom.

I guess I have to face it, that god is dead, we are chasing phantoms. There is no one left to save me from the [[Groups|kingdom]] I came from, because I am all alone in this bottomless pit.

As my body is being torn apart, I wanted to leave a final farewell message. I hope it reaches someone who shows no mercy to themselves, I hope it can save another life, because for me, it's too late.




{{Collapse|num=1|initial='''...'''|final=|body=


My life is nothing but misery; I am nothing more than a blinded prisoner. After enduring a ever lasting educational ordeal, I became a slave to a scoundrel in pursuit of financial freedom, and the rest of my old life was spent in endless, [[Level 4|unoriginal office rooms]]. My second life happened suddenly. I thought fate had chosen me to be one of the special ones. How ironic. My journey was again spent [[Level 0|hearing the cries of exploited workers]] and [[Level 1|their cries of selfhood]]. At the end of the day, I made a few friends. There were actually a few wanderers wandering in [[Level 2|the tunnels]]. Now that I think about it, I was one of them in my previous life, too. After realizing I was being exploited at work, I was fired. What else could I expect? From that day on, I started living in the tunnels. Darkness was my home. I experienced what lay behind the mask Vegas wore. For years, I was completely alone in the tunnels beneath the city. I collected things people discarded, trying to find my way in the [[Level 6|pitch black]]. Even though I met people over time, what can you do? Everyone is after their own interests. All my misery was washed away in the flood disaster. It was painful to see people slowly dying. At that moment, I don't know how, but I ended up here. But a wound remained inside me. The ease with which human life was discarded tore me apart. So I decided to befriend these tunnel dwellers, hoping they would bring good fortune to my second life. Our bond grew stronger at [[Level 3|the station]]; looking out for each other made us a kingdom. We lived like kings together. For the first time in my life, I felt loved and respected. When we reached [[Level 4|the offices]], most of the team members left us, saying they would no longer continue. I was furious. How could a person abandon their loved ones just for comfort? I continued with those who were worthy, until we fell into [[Level 6|darkness]]. We were completely lost. The kingdom of those who continued had been destroyed. I feel my journey will end here. Soon, I too will decay. Such a miserable blow to such a kingdom, such a wretched end befitting such a king. But my fate was sealed from the start. It turns out I was one of the [[Phenomenon 35|wretches]] we saw along the way.


[[File:Wretchnew2.png|400px|center|link=]]


}}
